title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured

input_row.title: Ensure that encryption providers are appropriately configured
input_row.description: Where etcd encryption is used, appropriate providers should be configured.
input_row.rationale: Where etcd encryption is used, it is important to ensure that the appropriate set of encryption providers is used. Currently, the aescbc, kms and secretbox are likely to be appropriate options. Impact: None
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Get the EncryptionConfig file set for --encryption-provider-config argument. Verify that aescbc, kms or secretbox is set as the encryption provider for all the desired resources.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and configure a EncryptionConfig file. In this file, choose aescbc, kms or secretbox as the encryption provider. Default Value: By default, no encryption provider is set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/ 2. https://acotten.com/post/kube17-security 3. https://kubernetes.io/docs/admin/kube-apiserver/ 4. https://github.com/kubernetes/features/issues/92 5. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/administer-cluster/encrypt-data/#providers

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_etcd_encryption_providers_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the kube-apiserver configuration to determine if the --encryption-provider-config flag is set.
- Retrieve the referenced EncryptionConfig file path from the kube-apiserver manifest (commonly located at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml or via the Kubernetes API if the control plane is managed as static pods).
- Parse the EncryptionConfig file to enumerate all configured resources and their associated encryption providers.
- Validate that for each resource listed, the encryption provider is one of: aescbc, kms, or secretbox.
- Pass Criteria:
  - The --encryption-provider-config flag is set on the kube-apiserver.
  - The EncryptionConfig file exists and is referenced correctly.
  - All resources in the EncryptionConfig file use only aescbc, kms, or secretbox as their encryption providers.
- Fail Criteria:
  - The --encryption-provider-config flag is missing.
  - The EncryptionConfig file is missing or not referenced.
  - Any resource uses an unsupported provider (e.g., identity or other non-encrypting providers).
- Example CLI commands:
  - `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o yaml` (to find the manifest and args)
  - `cat /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml | grep encryption-provider-config`
  - `cat <EncryptionConfig file>` and parse for provider types.
- This check can be fully automated as it relies on static configuration files and kube-apiserver arguments, both accessible via Kubernetes APIs or node file inspection in managed environments.
